segments.title: Prepping The Ramekins
steps: Butter the insides of four 6-ounce ramekins using the ['ing']{Unsalted butter}. Be generous and brush upward strokes to encourage the soufflé to rise. Add the ['ing']{Granulated sugar} and tilt to coat evenly, then tip out any excess.

segments.title: Soufflé Base & Meringue
steps: In a heatproof bowl, combine the ['ing']{Unsalted butter} and the ['ing']{Semi-sweet chocolate}. Melt over a double boiler or in the microwave in 20-second bursts, stirring until smooth. Let cool slightly, then whisk in the ['ing']{Large egg yolks}, ['ing']{Pure vanilla extract}, and ['ing']{Pinch of salt} until combined.

In a clean, grease-free bowl, using an electric mixer, beat the ['ing']{Large egg whites} and ['ing']{Cream of Tartar} on medium-high speed until soft peaks form. Gradually add the ['ing']{Granulated sugar}, about 1 tbsp at a time, and continue beating until stiff, glossy peaks form.
Gently fold about 1/4 of the meringue into the chocolate base to lighten it. Then, carefully fold in the remaining meringue until just combined and no large white streaks remain.
Divide the batter evenly among the prepared ramekins, smoothing the tops. Run your thumb around the inside rim of each ramekin to create a "moat." This helps the soufflés rise straight up.
Bake immediately on a baking sheet in a preheated ['temp']{190} oven for 14-16 minutes, or until puffed and the tops are set. Serve immediately.
